reasons: Diggie can reduce Minotaur's main teamfight value by protecting allies from crowd-control chains.
Minotaur relies on AoE engage, so Time Journey can deny the most important setup window.

proof.summary: Minotaur wants to lock multiple enemies with AoE crowd control, while Diggie can protect grouped allies during the engage window.
worksBestWhen: Diggie saves Time Journey for Minoan Fury or the real stomp engage.
Allies are close enough to receive protection.
failureCases: Minotaur baits Time Journey with a fake engage.
Diggie is caught or zoned before the fight starts.

reasons: Baxia reduces Minotaur's healing value in extended fights.
Anti-heal pressure makes it harder for Minotaur's team to stabilize after engage.

proof.summary: Minotaur wants to heal and extend teamfights, while Baxia can reduce the healing value so the enemy team has less time to stabilize.
worksBestWhen: Baxia applies anti-heal before Minotaur's team receives follow-up sustain.
Allies commit damage while healing value is reduced.
failureCases: Baxia cannot reach the grouped enemy team.
Minotaur wins the engage before anti-heal matters.

reasons: Valir can push Minotaur away before Minotaur reaches a strong engage angle.
Knockback and slow pressure make Minotaur's approach more predictable and easier to reset.

proof.summary: Minotaur wants to enter the middle of a teamfight, while Valir can deny approach angles with knockback and slow before Minotaur reaches the backline.
worksBestWhen: Valir holds knockback for Minotaur's entry.
Valir stays outside Despair Stomp range.
failureCases: Minotaur engages from fog before Valir reacts.
Valir wastes knockback on another target.

reasons: Karrie can pressure Minotaur's tank durability with repeated tank-shred damage.
Minotaur struggles when he cannot survive long enough to cycle healing and control.

proof.summary: Minotaur wants to absorb damage and control space, while Karrie can wear him down if she has protection and attack uptime.
worksBestWhen: Karrie has peel against Minotaur's engage.
Karrie attacks from safe distance during the frontline exchange.
failureCases: Minotaur catches Karrie with AoE control.
Karrie is zoned before she can stack damage.

reasons: Dyrroth can reduce Minotaur's durability and punish him before extended sustain matters.
Defense reduction and burst pressure help break through Minotaur's frontline role.

proof.summary: Minotaur wants to survive long enough to engage, heal, and control the fight, while Dyrroth can reduce his durability and force a faster kill window.
worksBestWhen: Dyrroth engages when Minotaur's main control is unavailable.
Allies follow up on the defense-reduction window.
failureCases: Minotaur catches Dyrroth with crowd control first.
Dyrroth dives without enough team damage.
